description: Strategically sound and legally durable claims — appropriate scope, independent/dependent architecture, differentiation from prior art, correct Markush groups where applicable, antecedent basis hygiene.
sub_criteria: Independent-claim scope appropriateness (too broad = unenforceable; too narrow = designed-around)
Independent / dependent claim architecture depth and hierarchy
Differentiation from cited prior art (§103 nonobviousness-aware drafting)
Markush group correctness where applicable (chemistry, mechanical)
Antecedent basis hygiene (every 'the [noun]' has a prior definite introduction)

scoring_levels.label: Excellent
scoring_levels.criteria: Independent claim drafted at the broadest scope supported by the disclosure that does not read on cited prior art; dependent chain progressively narrows along strategically valuable axes (structural, functional, jurisdictional fallback positions); Markush groups, where used, are closed and properly bounded; zero antecedent-basis defects across the claim set; differentiation over cited art is explicit and §103-aware.

scoring_levels.label: Good
scoring_levels.criteria: Strong claim set with minor scope or hierarchy issues that an experienced patent attorney could close in a single editing pass. Differentiation over cited art is present. Antecedent-basis defects, if any, are limited to one or two cosmetic instances (e.g., 'the device' before its first introduction in a single dependent claim).

scoring_levels.label: Adequate
scoring_levels.criteria: Claims are filable and would receive a Notice of Allowance after standard prosecution, but scope is suboptimal: either too cautious (leaves enforceable scope on the table) or too aggressive (will trigger §102/§103 rejections requiring substantial amendment). Dependent chain is shallow or non-strategic.

scoring_levels.label: Weak
scoring_levels.criteria: Material scope problems: missing independent claims for distinct statutory categories (apparatus / method / system) where the disclosure would support them; or independent claim reads on the cited prior art directly. Systematic antecedent-basis errors across multiple claims. Markush groups, where used, are open-ended or improperly bounded.

scoring_levels.label: Unacceptable
scoring_levels.criteria: Claims would not survive USPTO formality review (mixed statutory categories within a single claim, hybrid apparatus/method, 'means for' without §112(f) structural support). Independent claim is anticipated by a cited reference. Structural defects render the claim set unenforceable as drafted.

track_a_signals: BERTScore against ground-truth independent claims for the same disclosure
Coverage ratio of survived claim limitations present in candidate draft
Independent / dependent claim count alignment with ground truth
track_b_protocol: Reviewer reads candidate draft + disclosure (without prior-art references), scores 1-5 against sub-criteria, then provides forced-rank across all candidates for the same invention.
